source: Official GGG forum mechanical terminology + skill tag behavior discussions

Definition.text: Projectile denotes a tagged damage vector that conceptually travels along a path, with non-projectile skills unable to use projectile mechanics such as piercing.

Purpose.text: Route path-travel effects, travel-distance modifiers, and projectile interactions through the projectile tag pipeline.

Core Rules.text: Projectile-specific modifiers apply only when the skill/effect has projectile designation; projectile and area interactions are distinct unless both tags are present.

Internal Rules.text: Path of Exile discussions confirm `projectile` and `projectile attack` are separate tags; `projectile` affects all projectile damage including spells, while `projectile attack damage` is attack-only.

Calculation Order.text: 1) determine skill/effect tags 2) apply projectile-only checks (for example piercing/point-blank interactions) 3) apply Hit damage pipeline and source-specific scaling.

Priority Rules.text: If a mechanic is labeled projectile-specific, it does not apply to non-projectile components even when both arise from one skill.

Preconditions.text: Mechanic references a travel action or a projectile keyword explicitly set by skill text, support logic, or tag classification.

Postconditions.text: Tagged projectile components can receive projectile speed or projectile damage scaling and can carry separate damage sub-tags.

Limitations.text: Not all AoE instances are projectiles; some AoE skills still cannot be modified by projectile-only mechanics.

Caps.text: No standalone numeric cap on projectile keyword.

Formula.text: No single universal formula for Projectile; apply dedicated projectile modifiers in the projectile-metric stack alongside base Hit/DoT pipelines.
Formula.source: Path of Exile mechanic tag behavior

cannot_affect: AoE only effects without projectile tag

text: AoE-labeled components mistaken as Projectile when no Projectile tag exists.
Skill output can include both Projectile and AoE tags; components remain separable for interaction checks.

Hidden Rules.text: Store tags on a per-component basis (for example, explosion portion vs initial projectile hit).

Developer Notes.text: Prefer explicit component tagging (`projectile`, `projectile attack`, `AoE`) in schema and avoid merging by visual expectation.

Historical Changes.text: No cross-version redefinition of the core projectile tag distinction was captured in this pass.
Historical Changes.source: official forum snapshots in run window

text.note: Projectile tag remains a travel/interaction path distinct from AoE-only components.

text: Fireball (projectile hit) can also include AoE explosion component.
Shrapnel Shot cone does not itself count as Projectile and cannot use pierce.
All bow damage is automatically projectile regardless of explicit skill tag.
